title: Roomba Is Stuck with a Wheel Hanging Over a Cliff
shortDescription: Roomba has detected one of its wheels hanging over a drop-off or cliff edge.
fullDescription: Error 1 occurs when Roomba's cliff sensors detect that one or more wheels are suspended over a ledge or stairway. The robot stops to prevent a fall. This can also trigger on dark-colored floors or rugs that the infrared cliff sensors interpret as a drop-off. The robot will announce the error with 1 beep or display Error 1 on the app.

causes.cause: Roomba is genuinely stuck on a ledge or stair edge
Cliff sensors are dirty or obstructed by dust and debris
Dark-colored flooring or rugs triggering false cliff detection
Cliff sensor hardware failure

symptoms: Roomba stops moving and beeps once
Error 1 displayed in iRobot Home app
Robot refuses to move from its current position
Roomba announces it is stuck near a cliff
Repeated stops on dark-colored carpet or rugs

fixSteps.title: Move Roomba to a safe location
fixSteps.instruction: Pick up Roomba and place it on a flat surface away from stairs or ledges. Press the CLEAN button to restart the cleaning cycle.

fixSteps.title: Clean the cliff sensors
fixSteps.instruction: Turn Roomba over and locate the four cliff sensors on the underside near the front bumper edge. Wipe each sensor window with a clean, dry microfiber cloth or use compressed air to remove dust buildup.

fixSteps.title: Check for dark flooring triggers
fixSteps.instruction: If the error occurs on dark floors or rugs, the infrared sensors may interpret the dark surface as a cliff. Try covering the cliff sensors with white tape temporarily to test. If confirmed, use virtual walls or Keep Out Zones to avoid those areas.

fixSteps.title: Inspect cliff sensor wiring
fixSteps.instruction: If cleaning does not help, remove the bottom plate (6 screws) and inspect the cliff sensor ribbon cables for damage or loose connections. Reseat all connectors firmly.

whenToCallPro: If cleaning sensors and reseating cables does not resolve the issue, a cliff sensor module replacement may be needed.
videoSearchQuery: iRobot Roomba Error 1 cliff sensor fix

faq.q: Why does my Roomba keep saying Error 1 on dark floors?
faq.a: Roomba's infrared cliff sensors can mistake very dark surfaces for drop-offs. This is a known limitation. Use Keep Out Zones in the app to prevent Roomba from entering those areas.

faq.q: How do I clean the cliff sensors on my Roomba?
faq.a: Turn Roomba upside down and wipe the four small sensor windows near the front edge with a dry microfiber cloth. Do this weekly for best results.

faq.q: Can I disable the cliff sensors?
faq.a: iRobot does not provide an official way to disable cliff sensors. Some users cover them with tape, but this removes fall protection and is done at your own risk.

title: Main Brushes Cannot Spin
shortDescription: The main brush extractors are stalled or obstructed and cannot rotate.
fullDescription: Error 2 indicates that the dual multi-surface rubber extractors (or bristle brushes on older models) cannot spin. Hair, string, carpet fibers, or other debris has become tangled around the brushes or their end caps, preventing rotation. The motor that drives the brushes detects the stall and triggers this error to protect itself from overheating.

causes.cause: Hair and string wrapped around brush extractors
Debris lodged in brush end caps or bearings
Brush extractor bearings worn out
Brush motor failure

symptoms: Roomba stops and beeps twice
Error 2 message in the iRobot Home app
Brushes visibly jammed with hair or debris
Grinding or clicking noise before the error
Reduced cleaning performance before complete stall

fixSteps.title: Remove the brush extractors
fixSteps.instruction: Flip Roomba over and press the yellow tabs on either side of the brush frame to release it. Pull both rubber extractors out of the housing.

fixSteps.title: Remove tangled hair and debris
fixSteps.instruction: Pull all hair, string, and fibers from the extractors. Remove the end caps on each extractor and clean the square axle and round bearing area thoroughly. A seam ripper or scissors helps cut tangled hair.

fixSteps.title: Clean the brush housing
fixSteps.instruction: Use a dry cloth or compressed air to clean the brush housing cavity. Remove any debris from the hex posts and gear teeth inside the frame.

fixSteps.title: Replace worn brush extractors
fixSteps.instruction: If the extractors are visibly worn, torn, or deformed, replace them with genuine iRobot replacements. Insert new extractors ensuring the green and yellow ends align with the correct sides.

whenToCallPro: Only if the brush motor itself has failed and brushes still do not spin after removing all obstructions and replacing extractors.
videoSearchQuery: Roomba Error 2 brush extractor cleaning how to fix

faq.q: How often should I clean the Roomba brushes?
faq.a: iRobot recommends removing and cleaning the extractors once per week, or more often if you have pets that shed heavily.

faq.q: Can I use third-party replacement brushes?
faq.a: Third-party brushes are available at lower cost, but fit and durability can vary. iRobot genuine extractors are recommended for best performance.

faq.q: Why does Error 2 happen so frequently?
faq.a: Long hair and pet fur are the most common culprits. Cleaning extractors weekly and removing end cap buildup prevents most occurrences.

title: Side Brush Stalled
shortDescription: The side brush cannot rotate due to an obstruction or motor issue.
fullDescription: Error 3 is triggered when the side brush motor detects a stall condition. The three-pronged side brush is responsible for sweeping debris from edges and corners into the path of the main brushes. Hair, thread, or debris can wrap around the side brush axle, preventing rotation.

causes.cause: Hair or thread wrapped around the side brush axle
Side brush screw is over-tightened, preventing free rotation
Side brush module gear stripped or worn
Side brush motor failure

symptoms: Roomba beeps three times and stops
Error 3 displayed in the app
Side brush is visibly not spinning during operation
Clicking or grinding sound from the side brush area
Poor edge cleaning performance

fixSteps.title: Remove the side brush
fixSteps.instruction: Flip Roomba over. Use a Phillips screwdriver to remove the single screw holding the side brush. Pull the brush off the axle.

fixSteps.title: Clean the axle and brush
fixSteps.instruction: Remove all hair, string, and debris wrapped around the axle post and the underside of the brush. Use scissors to cut away stubborn tangles.

fixSteps.title: Check the axle spins freely
fixSteps.instruction: With the brush removed, gently spin the axle post with your finger. It should rotate smoothly. If it feels gritty or stuck, apply a small drop of light machine oil to the axle.

fixSteps.title: Replace the side brush if damaged
fixSteps.instruction: If the brush bristles are bent or the mounting hole is stripped, replace the side brush. Reattach with the screw, tightening until snug but not over-tight so the brush can still spin freely.

whenToCallPro: If the side brush module motor has failed, the module must be replaced, which requires partial disassembly of the robot.
videoSearchQuery: Roomba Error 3 side brush fix cleaning

faq.q: How often should I clean the side brush?
faq.a: Clean the side brush weekly by removing it and clearing any tangled hair. Replace it every 6-12 months or when the bristles become frayed.

faq.q: Why does my Roomba side brush keep getting tangled?
faq.a: Long hair and pet fur naturally wrap around the spinning axle. Regular weekly cleaning is the best prevention.

faq.q: Can I run Roomba without the side brush?
faq.a: Technically yes, but edge and corner cleaning performance will be significantly reduced.

title: Left or Right Wheel Stalled
shortDescription: One of Roomba's drive wheels is stuck and cannot rotate.
fullDescription: Error 5 indicates that one of Roomba's main drive wheels is unable to spin. This can be caused by debris lodged around the wheel axle, a stuck wheel, hair tangled in the wheel mechanism, or a worn wheel module. Roomba relies on differential drive (two independently powered wheels) for navigation, so a stuck wheel halts all movement.

causes.cause: Debris or hair wrapped around the wheel axle
Wheel physically stuck against furniture or an obstacle
Wheel module spring mechanism compressed or broken
Drive wheel motor failure

symptoms: Roomba beeps five times and stops
Error 5 shown in the iRobot Home app
One wheel appears stuck or does not spin freely by hand
Roomba spins in circles before stopping
Clicking noise from one wheel area

fixSteps.title: Free the stuck wheel
fixSteps.instruction: Pick up Roomba and check both wheels. Push each wheel in and out to test the spring mechanism. Spin each wheel by hand to check for free rotation. Remove any visible debris or hair.

fixSteps.title: Clean the wheel cavities
fixSteps.instruction: Turn Roomba upside down and use compressed air or a thin brush to clean around both wheel wells. Hair and dust accumulate in the axle area where the wheel enters the body.

fixSteps.title: Test wheel spring action
fixSteps.instruction: Push each wheel firmly up into the body and release. Both wheels should spring back down smoothly and evenly. If one wheel feels stiff or does not return, the wheel module may need cleaning or replacement.

fixSteps.title: Replace the wheel module
fixSteps.instruction: If the wheel remains stuck, remove the bottom plate, disconnect the wheel module connector from the main board, remove the 3 retaining screws, and pull out the wheel module. Install the replacement and reconnect.

whenToCallPro: If the wheel motor has failed internally, the entire wheel module must be replaced.
videoSearchQuery: Roomba Error 5 wheel stuck fix replacement

faq.q: Why does only one wheel get stuck?
faq.a: Hair and debris tend to accumulate more on one side depending on your home layout and the direction Roomba navigates most often.

faq.q: How do I know which wheel is causing Error 5?
faq.a: Spin both wheels by hand. The stuck one will feel stiff, not rotate freely, or not spring back when pushed in.

faq.q: Can I lubricate the Roomba wheels?
faq.a: You can apply a tiny amount of silicone lubricant to the axle, but avoid petroleum-based oils as they can degrade plastic components.

title: Cliff Sensors Are Dirty or Roomba Is Stuck
shortDescription: Roomba's cliff sensors are obstructed or the robot is dangling over an edge.
fullDescription: Error 6 is similar to Error 1 but specifically indicates persistent cliff sensor issues. All four cliff sensors are reading as though Roomba is over a cliff, suggesting either all sensors are dirty, Roomba is genuinely stuck on a ledge, or there is a wiring issue with the cliff sensor harness.

causes.cause: All cliff sensors are covered with dust or grime
Roomba is stuck on a raised surface with wheels dangling
Cliff sensor ribbon cable loose or damaged
Multiple cliff sensor failures

symptoms: Roomba beeps six times and will not move
Error 6 in the iRobot Home app
Robot refuses to start cleaning from any location
Works briefly then immediately stops with Error 6

fixSteps.title: Relocate Roomba
fixSteps.instruction: Pick up Roomba and place it in the center of a flat, light-colored floor. Press CLEAN to restart.

fixSteps.title: Thoroughly clean all cliff sensors
fixSteps.instruction: Flip Roomba over. Locate all four cliff sensors along the front edge of the underside. Clean each sensor window with a slightly damp microfiber cloth followed by a dry wipe. Use compressed air to blow out any dust in the recesses.

fixSteps.title: Test on different floor types
fixSteps.instruction: Place Roomba on a light-colored hard floor and test. If it works there but not on dark carpet, the sensors are misreading dark surfaces. Use virtual barriers to exclude problem areas.

fixSteps.title: Check internal sensor connections
fixSteps.instruction: Remove the bottom plate and locate the cliff sensor ribbon cable connecting to the main board. Reseat the connector firmly. Inspect for any visible damage to the cable.

whenToCallPro: If cleaning and reseating connections does not resolve the error, the cliff sensor board may need replacement.
videoSearchQuery: Roomba Error 6 cliff sensors cleaning fix

faq.q: What is the difference between Error 1 and Error 6?
faq.a: Error 1 means one sensor detected a cliff. Error 6 means multiple sensors are all reading as cliffs simultaneously, usually indicating dirty sensors rather than an actual ledge.

faq.q: How often should cliff sensors be cleaned?
faq.a: Clean the cliff sensors at least once a week, especially if you have pets or dusty environments.

faq.q: Can Error 6 damage my Roomba?
faq.a: No, Error 6 is a safety feature that prevents Roomba from moving. It will not cause damage but does prevent cleaning until resolved.

title: Wheel Drop Error
shortDescription: One or both drive wheels have dropped out of contact with the floor.
fullDescription: Error 7 indicates that Roomba's wheel drop sensors detect that one or both wheels are not in their normal extended position. This can occur if Roomba is lifted off the floor, if a wheel is physically stuck in the retracted position, or if the wheel drop sensor is malfunctioning.

causes.cause: Roomba has been picked up while running
Drive wheel stuck in retracted position
Debris preventing wheel from extending fully
Wheel drop sensor malfunction

symptoms: Roomba beeps seven times
Error 7 in the app
Wheels appear stuck in the up position
Roomba will not move even on flat floor

fixSteps.title: Place Roomba on flat ground
fixSteps.instruction: Set Roomba on a flat, hard surface. Push each wheel down firmly and release. They should spring back smoothly.

fixSteps.title: Clean around wheel wells
fixSteps.instruction: Use compressed air to blow out debris from both wheel cavities. Push each wheel up and down several times to dislodge any trapped debris.

fixSteps.title: Check for broken spring mechanism
fixSteps.instruction: Press each wheel into the body and let go. If a wheel does not spring back, the internal spring is broken or the wheel module needs replacement.

fixSteps.title: Replace wheel module
fixSteps.instruction: Remove the bottom plate, unscrew the 3 screws on the faulty wheel module, disconnect it from the main board, and install a new module.

whenToCallPro: If the wheel module spring is broken or the wheel drop sensor has failed internally.
videoSearchQuery: Roomba Error 7 wheel drop fix

faq.q: Why does Roomba show Error 7 when it is on the floor?
faq.a: The wheel drop sensor may be stuck. Push both wheels firmly up and down several times to free them, then clean around the wheel wells.

faq.q: Is Error 7 dangerous?
faq.a: No, it is a safety stop that prevents Roomba from operating when it thinks it is being held. Simply placing it on the floor usually resolves it.

faq.q: What triggers wheel drop detection?
faq.a: Small switches inside the wheel wells detect whether the wheels are under load. If a wheel is not touching the floor, the switch is released, triggering the error.

title: Vacuum Motor Stalled or Suction Problem
shortDescription: The vacuum motor cannot operate or suction is blocked.
fullDescription: Error 8 indicates the vacuum motor or fan is unable to spin, or the motor current draw is abnormally high due to a blockage. This can be caused by a clogged filter, full dustbin, blocked air pathway, or a failed vacuum motor impeller.

causes.cause: Dustbin is full and obstructing airflow
Filter is clogged with fine dust
Suction pathway blocked by large debris
Vacuum motor impeller damaged or seized

symptoms: Roomba beeps eight times
Error 8 in the app
Noticeably reduced suction before error
High-pitched whine from vacuum motor
Dustbin area feels hot

fixSteps.title: Empty the dustbin
fixSteps.instruction: Remove the dustbin by pressing the release button. Empty all debris into a trash can. Tap the bin firmly to dislodge compacted dust.

fixSteps.title: Clean or replace the filter
fixSteps.instruction: Remove the filter from the dustbin. Tap it against a trash can to remove dust. For HEPA-style filters, do NOT wash with water. Replace the filter if it is visibly gray or has been used for more than 2 months.

fixSteps.title: Check for pathway blockages
fixSteps.instruction: Look into the dustbin intake port and the suction channel on Roomba's underside. Remove any large debris, socks, or paper that may be blocking the airflow path.

fixSteps.title: Inspect the vacuum motor fan
fixSteps.instruction: If the error persists, remove the dustbin and look into the fan area. Try spinning the fan gently with a cotton swab. If it does not spin freely, the motor may need replacement.

whenToCallPro: If the vacuum motor fan is seized or making abnormal sounds after clearing all blockages.
videoSearchQuery: Roomba Error 8 vacuum suction fix filter cleaning

faq.q: How often should I replace the Roomba filter?
faq.a: iRobot recommends replacing the filter every 2 months, or sooner if it appears visibly dirty or gray.

faq.q: Can I wash the Roomba filter?
faq.a: Most Roomba filters are not washable. Tapping them against a trash can to dislodge dust is the recommended cleaning method. Using water can damage the filter material.

faq.q: Why does my Roomba keep losing suction?
faq.a: The most common cause is a dirty filter. Empty the bin after every use and replace filters regularly.

title: Bumper Is Stuck or Sensor Dirty
shortDescription: The front bumper sensor is compressed or dirty, making Roomba think it is constantly hitting an obstacle.
fullDescription: Error 9 occurs when the front bumper appears to be permanently depressed or the IR bumper sensors are sending constant collision signals. The bumper must spring back freely after contact for normal navigation. Debris, sticky residue, or a misaligned bumper can prevent proper operation.

causes.cause: Debris lodged between bumper and robot body
Bumper physically stuck in depressed position
Sticky residue on bumper contact points
Bumper IR sensor dirty or failed

symptoms: Roomba beeps nine times
Error 9 in the app
Bumper does not spring back when pressed
Roomba drives backward repeatedly
Roomba keeps turning as if hitting invisible walls

fixSteps.title: Tap and flex the bumper
fixSteps.instruction: Tap the bumper firmly around its entire circumference about 10-20 times to dislodge any trapped debris. Push and release the bumper to ensure it springs back freely.

fixSteps.title: Clean the bumper gap
fixSteps.instruction: Use compressed air to blow into the gap between the bumper and the main body all the way around. Wipe the bumper surface with a slightly damp cloth to remove any sticky residue.

fixSteps.title: Clean bumper IR sensors
fixSteps.instruction: Look at the bumper face for the IR sensor windows (small dark areas). Wipe them with a dry microfiber cloth to clear dust that could cause false readings.

fixSteps.title: Reboot Roomba
fixSteps.instruction: Hold the CLEAN button for 10 seconds until Roomba resets (light ring will swirl). This clears sensor states. For older models, remove the battery for 60 seconds.

whenToCallPro: If the bumper is physically cracked or the IR sensor board behind the bumper has failed.
videoSearchQuery: Roomba Error 9 bumper stuck fix

faq.q: Why does Roomba think it is always bumping into something?
faq.a: Debris trapped in the bumper gap or a sticky bumper can make the sensor think it is constantly compressed. Cleaning the gap resolves most cases.

faq.q: Can a stuck bumper damage the robot?
faq.a: Not directly, but it prevents normal navigation and cleaning. The robot will keep trying to back up and turn, wasting battery.

faq.q: How do I know if the bumper springs back properly?
faq.a: Press the bumper in at several points around its circumference. It should bounce back immediately and evenly at all points.

title: Side Wheel Stuck or Bumper Stuck
shortDescription: A side wheel or the front bumper is obstructed.
fullDescription: Error 10 is a combined wheel-and-bumper error. The robot has detected that either a drive wheel or the bumper is persistently stuck. This prevents Roomba from navigating safely.

causes.cause: One drive wheel jammed by debris
Bumper stuck in depressed position
Roomba wedged under low furniture
Wheel or bumper sensor fault

symptoms: Roomba beeps ten times
Error 10 displayed in app
Robot stuck under furniture
Wheel or bumper visibly jammed

fixSteps.title: Free the robot
fixSteps.instruction: Pick up Roomba and move it to an open area on a flat floor.

fixSteps.title: Check wheels and bumper
fixSteps.instruction: Push each wheel up and down to test the spring. Press and release the bumper all the way around. Clean any debris from wheel wells and the bumper gap.

fixSteps.title: Use virtual barriers
fixSteps.instruction: If Roomba repeatedly gets stuck under specific furniture, set up virtual walls or Keep Out Zones in the app to block those areas.

fixSteps.title: Reboot Roomba
fixSteps.instruction: Hold the CLEAN button for 10 seconds to perform a soft reset. Place Roomba on the Home Base to charge fully before trying again.

whenToCallPro: If the error persists after cleaning all mechanical components.
videoSearchQuery: Roomba Error 10 stuck wheel bumper fix

faq.q: What is the difference between Error 5 and Error 10?
faq.a: Error 5 is specifically a wheel stall. Error 10 can be either a wheel or bumper issue combined.

faq.q: How do I prevent Roomba from getting stuck under furniture?
faq.a: Measure the height clearance under furniture. If it is close to Roomba's height, use virtual walls or physical barriers to block access.

faq.q: Does Error 10 mean my Roomba is broken?
faq.a: Usually not. It most often means the robot got physically stuck. Moving it to an open area and cleaning the wheels and bumper resolves most cases.

title: Vacuum Has an Internal Error
shortDescription: An internal communication or hardware fault has been detected.
fullDescription: Error 11 is an internal system error indicating a communication failure between Roomba's main processor and one of its subsystems. This could be a temporary glitch or a sign of a failing internal component.

causes.cause: Temporary firmware glitch
Internal ribbon cable loose or disconnected
Main board communication error
Failed internal component

symptoms: Roomba beeps eleven times
Error 11 in the app
Robot stops mid-cycle for no apparent physical reason
Intermittent shutdowns during cleaning

fixSteps.title: Reboot Roomba
fixSteps.instruction: Hold the CLEAN button for 10 seconds until the light ring goes off. Wait 10 seconds, then press CLEAN again to restart.

fixSteps.title: Perform a factory reset
fixSteps.instruction: For i-series and j-series: hold the Home, Spot Clean, and CLEAN buttons simultaneously for 20 seconds. For 600/800/900 series: hold CLEAN for 10 seconds. This resets all software settings.

fixSteps.title: Update firmware
fixSteps.instruction: Ensure Roomba is connected to Wi-Fi. Open the iRobot Home app and check for firmware updates under Product Settings. Install any available updates.

fixSteps.title: Check internal connections
fixSteps.instruction: If the error persists, remove the bottom plate and inspect all ribbon cables and connectors for looseness, corrosion, or damage. Reseat all connections.

whenToCallPro: If the error persists after factory reset and firmware update, indicating a hardware fault.
videoSearchQuery: Roomba Error 11 internal error fix factory reset

faq.q: Is Error 11 a serious problem?
faq.a: Often no. A simple reboot or factory reset resolves most Error 11 occurrences. It becomes serious only if it recurs frequently.

faq.q: Will a factory reset delete my maps?
faq.a: Yes. A factory reset clears all saved maps, preferences, and schedules. You will need to remap your home and reconfigure settings.

faq.q: How do I prevent Error 11?
faq.a: Keep firmware updated and ensure Roomba is charged on the Home Base regularly. Avoid interrupting Roomba during firmware updates.

title: Dustbin Not Detected
shortDescription: Roomba cannot detect the dustbin installed in the robot.
fullDescription: Error 14 occurs when the bin detection sensor cannot confirm that the dustbin is properly installed. This can be caused by a misaligned bin, dirty bin contacts, or a faulty bin detection switch.

causes.cause: Dustbin not fully seated in the robot
Bin detection contacts dirty
Dustbin latch or tabs broken
Bin detection sensor or switch failed

symptoms: Error 14 in the app
Roomba refuses to start cleaning
Dustbin appears properly installed but is not recognized
Intermittent Error 14 during cleaning

fixSteps.title: Reseat the dustbin
fixSteps.instruction: Remove the dustbin by pressing the release button. Check for any debris around the bin cavity. Reinsert the bin firmly until you hear or feel it click into place.

fixSteps.title: Clean the bin contacts
fixSteps.instruction: Locate the electrical contacts on both the dustbin and inside the bin cavity on Roomba. Wipe them with a dry microfiber cloth or isopropyl alcohol to remove dust and oxidation.

fixSteps.title: Inspect the bin latch
fixSteps.instruction: Check that the dustbin's latch mechanism and alignment tabs are not cracked or broken. If tabs are broken, the bin cannot seat properly.

fixSteps.title: Try a different dustbin
fixSteps.instruction: If available, try installing a different compatible dustbin to determine if the issue is the bin or the robot's sensor.

whenToCallPro: If the bin detection switch or sensor on the robot body has failed.
videoSearchQuery: Roomba Error 14 bin not detected fix

faq.q: Can I run Roomba without the dustbin?
faq.a: No. Roomba requires the dustbin to be detected before it will start. The bin forms part of the suction pathway.

faq.q: Will a Clean Base dustbin work for this?
faq.a: The disposable Clean Base bags are different from the standard dustbin. Make sure you are using the correct bin for your model.

faq.q: Why does the bin keep disconnecting during cleaning?
faq.a: Vibration during operation can jostle a bin that is not fully latched. Ensure the bin clicks firmly into place and the latch is not worn.

title: Internal Communication Error
shortDescription: Roomba has experienced a persistent internal communication failure.
fullDescription: Error 15 indicates a repeated internal communication failure that was not resolved by a single reboot. This is a more persistent version of Error 11 and may indicate a hardware problem with the main board or sensor bus.

causes.cause: Persistent firmware bug requiring update
Damaged internal communication bus
Main board failure
Water or liquid damage to internal components

symptoms: Error 15 in the app after multiple reboots
Robot stops repeatedly during cleaning cycles
Error occurs immediately on startup
Previous Error 11 escalated to Error 15

fixSteps.title: Factory reset
fixSteps.instruction: Perform a full factory reset: for i/j series, hold Home + Spot Clean + CLEAN for 20 seconds. For 600/800/900 series, hold CLEAN for 10 seconds.

fixSteps.title: Update firmware
fixSteps.instruction: Connect Roomba to Wi-Fi, dock it on the Home Base, and check for firmware updates in the iRobot Home app.

fixSteps.title: Inspect for water damage
fixSteps.instruction: Remove the bottom plate and inspect the main board for signs of water damage: corrosion, white or green residue on connectors, or staining on the board.

fixSteps.title: Contact iRobot support
fixSteps.instruction: If Error 15 persists, contact iRobot customer support. If under warranty, a replacement may be provided. Out of warranty, the main board likely needs professional replacement.

whenToCallPro: Immediately if factory reset and firmware update do not resolve the issue.
videoSearchQuery: Roomba Error 15 internal communication fix

faq.q: Is Error 15 covered under warranty?
faq.a: If your Roomba is within the warranty period (typically 1-2 years), iRobot will usually replace or repair it for Error 15 issues.

faq.q: Can water damage cause Error 15?
faq.a: Yes. If Roomba has run over a spill or wet area, moisture can reach the main board and cause communication failures.

faq.q: Should I try to repair Error 15 myself?
faq.a: Unless you have electronics repair experience, it is best to contact iRobot support. Main board replacement requires soldering skill.

title: Roomba Tilted on Uneven Surface
shortDescription: Roomba detects it is on an uneven surface and cannot navigate safely.
fullDescription: Error 16 indicates that Roomba's tilt sensor or accelerometer has detected that the robot is on a significantly uneven surface, making safe navigation impossible.

causes.cause: Roomba is on a heavily uneven or sloped surface
Robot stuck on top of an object or threshold
Accelerometer or tilt sensor malfunction
One wheel significantly lower than the other due to wear

symptoms: Error 16 in the app
Roomba stops on transitions between floor types
Robot refuses to start on certain areas of flooring
Error occurs on thick rugs or carpet edges

fixSteps.title: Move to flat surface
fixSteps.instruction: Pick up Roomba and place it on a flat, hard floor. Press CLEAN to restart.

fixSteps.title: Check for obstructions underneath
fixSteps.instruction: Turn Roomba over and check that nothing is stuck underneath that would cause it to sit unevenly. Check both wheels extend equally.

fixSteps.title: Avoid problem areas
fixSteps.instruction: If the error occurs at specific floor transitions or thick rug edges, use Keep Out Zones to avoid those areas.

fixSteps.title: Reboot the robot
fixSteps.instruction: Hold CLEAN for 10 seconds to reboot. This recalibrates the internal sensors.

whenToCallPro: If Roomba reports Error 16 on perfectly flat surfaces, the tilt sensor may be faulty.
videoSearchQuery: Roomba Error 16 tilted surface fix

faq.q: Can Roomba handle transitions between tile and carpet?
faq.a: Yes, for most normal transitions. Error 16 triggers only on severe slopes or very thick rug edges that tilt the robot significantly.

faq.q: What angle triggers Error 16?
faq.a: The exact threshold is not published by iRobot, but approximately 15-20 degrees of tilt will trigger the error.

faq.q: Why does Roomba tilt on my carpet?
faq.a: Very plush or shag carpets can cause one wheel to sink lower than the other, triggering the tilt sensor.

title: Navigation or Mapping Error
shortDescription: Roomba cannot navigate or has lost its position on the map.
fullDescription: Error 17 occurs when Roomba's navigation system (vSLAM camera or LIDAR depending on model) loses track of its position. The robot cannot determine where it is relative to its stored map and stops to prevent random movement.

causes.cause: Room is too dark for the vSLAM camera to navigate
Furniture has been significantly rearranged
vSLAM camera lens dirty or obstructed
Navigation camera hardware failure

symptoms: Error 17 in the app
Roomba stops and cannot find its way home
Random navigation patterns before stopping
Error occurs in dark rooms or at night

fixSteps.title: Ensure adequate lighting
fixSteps.instruction: Turn on lights in the rooms where Roomba operates. The vSLAM camera needs ambient light to navigate. Roomba cannot clean effectively in complete darkness.

fixSteps.title: Clean the camera lens
fixSteps.instruction: Locate the camera on top of Roomba (i-series, j-series) or the front (s9). Wipe the lens with a dry microfiber cloth.

fixSteps.title: Remap the home
fixSteps.instruction: If furniture has changed significantly, delete the stored map in the iRobot Home app and let Roomba create a new map during its next 2-3 cleaning runs.

fixSteps.title: Check for reflective surfaces
fixSteps.instruction: Mirrors, glass walls, and highly reflective surfaces near the floor can confuse the navigation camera. Rearrange or cover them if possible.

whenToCallPro: If the camera module has physically failed, requiring hardware replacement.
videoSearchQuery: Roomba Error 17 navigation mapping fix dark room

faq.q: Can Roomba clean in the dark?
faq.a: Models with vSLAM cameras (i, j, s series) require ambient light. Older 600 and 800 series use bump navigation and can clean in the dark.

faq.q: How do I delete and remap?
faq.a: In the iRobot Home app, go to Maps, select your map, and choose Delete Map. Roomba will build a new map over the next 2-3 cleaning runs.

faq.q: Does Error 17 lose my saved map?
faq.a: No, the saved map is retained. The error means Roomba temporarily lost its position within the existing map.

title: Roomba Cannot Return to Home Base
shortDescription: Roomba is unable to locate or dock with the Home Base.
fullDescription: Error 18 indicates that Roomba has been unable to navigate back to and dock with its Home Base or Clean Base. The infrared signal from the base may be obstructed, or the robot may be too far away to detect it.

causes.cause: Home Base IR signal blocked by objects
Home Base moved from its original position
Roomba's IR receiver dirty
Home Base IR emitter failing

symptoms: Error 18 in the app
Roomba wanders looking for base without docking
Robot stops in a random location with low battery
Roomba circles near the base but does not dock

fixSteps.title: Clear the area around the Home Base
fixSteps.instruction: Ensure there is at least 1.5 feet (50 cm) of clear space on each side of the base and 4 feet (1.2 m) in front. Remove any objects blocking the IR beam.

fixSteps.title: Verify Home Base placement
fixSteps.instruction: Place the Home Base against a flat wall on a hard surface. Ensure it is not on a rug that could shift. The base should not be in a corner or recessed area.

fixSteps.title: Clean the charging contacts
fixSteps.instruction: Wipe the metal charging contacts on both Roomba's underside and the Home Base with a dry microfiber cloth to remove dust and oxidation.

fixSteps.title: Clean Roomba's IR receiver
fixSteps.instruction: Wipe the front bumper area where the IR receiver is located. This is the window through which Roomba detects the Home Base signal.

whenToCallPro: If the Home Base IR emitter is failing or Roomba's IR receiver is damaged.
videoSearchQuery: Roomba Error 18 cannot find home base dock fix

faq.q: Where should I place the Home Base?
faq.a: On a hard, flat surface against a wall with 1.5 feet of clear space on each side and 4 feet in front. Avoid corners, glass walls, and areas with direct sunlight.

faq.q: Can sunlight interfere with docking?
faq.a: Yes. Direct sunlight shining on the Home Base or Roomba's bumper can interfere with the IR signals used for docking.

faq.q: Why does Roomba circle near the base but not dock?
faq.a: Dirty charging contacts or a misaligned base are the most common causes. Clean the contacts and ensure the base is flush against the wall.

title: Undocking Error
shortDescription: Roomba is having difficulty leaving the Home Base to start a cleaning mission.
fullDescription: Error 19 occurs when Roomba attempts to undock from the Home Base but fails. The robot may be stuck on the base, the wheels may not have traction, or the base is positioned in a way that prevents clean exit.

causes.cause: Home Base placed on slippery surface
Roomba wheels cannot grip the surface near the base
Objects blocking Roomba's exit path from the base
Drive wheel motor stalling under load

symptoms: Error 19 in the app
Roomba makes grinding sounds on the base but does not move
Robot partially undocks then returns to base
Scheduled cleanings fail to start

fixSteps.title: Secure the Home Base
fixSteps.instruction: Make sure the Home Base is on a non-slip surface. Use the anti-slip pad on the bottom if available. The base should not slide when Roomba drives off.

fixSteps.title: Clear the exit path
fixSteps.instruction: Ensure at least 4 feet of clear space in front of the base so Roomba can drive straight out.

fixSteps.title: Check wheel traction
fixSteps.instruction: Inspect both drive wheels for worn tread or debris that might reduce traction. Clean the wheels with a damp cloth.

fixSteps.title: Manually start the cleaning
fixSteps.instruction: Remove Roomba from the base, place it in the center of the room, and press CLEAN to start manually. If it cleans fine, the issue is specific to the undocking location.

whenToCallPro: Only if the drive wheels are mechanically failing to engage.
videoSearchQuery: Roomba Error 19 undocking problem fix

faq.q: Why can't Roomba leave the charging base?
faq.a: The most common cause is the base sliding on a smooth floor. Secure it with anti-slip material or place it on a rug.

faq.q: Does Error 19 mean Roomba is not charging?
faq.a: No, Error 19 is specifically about undocking. Roomba charges normally but cannot drive away from the base.

faq.q: Can I just start Roomba manually instead?
faq.a: Yes, you can always start a cleaning mission by pressing the CLEAN button or through the app after placing Roomba on the floor. However, it will still need to return to the base to recharge.

title: Low Memory or Map Error
shortDescription: Roomba does not have enough memory to complete navigation or store map data.
fullDescription: Error 22 indicates that Roomba's internal memory for mapping and navigation has reached capacity. This can occur in very large homes, or when the robot has accumulated corrupted map data over time.

causes.cause: Home is very large with many rooms exceeding map capacity
Corrupted map data consuming memory
Multiple saved maps filling storage
Firmware bug causing memory leak

symptoms: Error 22 in the app
Roomba cannot complete a full home cleaning
Map appears incomplete or corrupted in the app
Robot gets lost during long cleaning sessions

fixSteps.title: Delete old maps
fixSteps.instruction: Open the iRobot Home app, go to Maps, and delete any unused or outdated maps to free up memory.

fixSteps.title: Create a fresh map
fixSteps.instruction: Delete the current map and let Roomba create a new one. Start with one floor or section at a time.

fixSteps.title: Update firmware
fixSteps.instruction: Check for firmware updates in the iRobot Home app. Memory management improvements are often included in updates.

fixSteps.title: Factory reset
fixSteps.instruction: As a last resort, perform a factory reset to clear all stored data and start fresh.

whenToCallPro: Typically not needed. Contact iRobot support if the issue persists after factory reset.
videoSearchQuery: Roomba Error 22 map memory fix delete map

faq.q: How many maps can Roomba store?
faq.a: Most i and j series models can store up to 10 floor plans. The s9 can also store multiple maps.

faq.q: Will deleting a map lose my room labels?
faq.a: Yes. When you delete a map, all room labels, Keep Out Zones, and other customizations are lost. You will need to reconfigure them on the new map.

faq.q: Can Roomba clean a very large house?
faq.a: Roomba can map up to approximately 2,500-4,000 square feet per floor. For larger homes, you may need to clean in multiple sessions.

title: Vacuum Stall in Obstacle-Rich Area
shortDescription: Roomba is stuck in an area with too many obstacles to navigate.
fullDescription: Error 26 means Roomba has attempted to navigate through a heavily cluttered area and failed. The robot has bumped into obstacles repeatedly and cannot find a clear path forward or back to open space.

causes.cause: Room is too cluttered with cables, shoes, or small objects
Roomba trapped between chair legs or furniture clusters
Navigation confusion from many similar-looking obstacles
Camera or LIDAR sensor partially obstructed

symptoms: Error 26 in the app
Roomba stuck between objects
Robot makes many attempts to move, bumping repeatedly
Cleaning mission ends prematurely

fixSteps.title: Clear the area
fixSteps.instruction: Pick up Roomba and clear the floor of cables, shoes, toys, and small objects that could trap the robot.

fixSteps.title: Resume cleaning
fixSteps.instruction: Place Roomba in an open area and press CLEAN. It will attempt to resume the mission.

fixSteps.title: Set up Keep Out Zones
fixSteps.instruction: In the app, set Keep Out Zones around problem areas like cable clusters under desks or tight furniture arrangements.

fixSteps.title: Clean the navigation sensor
fixSteps.instruction: Wipe the camera lens or LIDAR turret with a dry microfiber cloth to ensure it has a clear view.

whenToCallPro: This error is almost always environmental. No professional repair is needed.
videoSearchQuery: Roomba Error 26 obstacle stuck fix

faq.q: How should I prepare my room for Roomba?
faq.a: Pick up cables, shoes, clothing, and small objects from the floor. Tuck cables behind furniture and lift curtains that drag on the floor.

faq.q: Can Roomba j7 avoid obstacles better?
faq.a: Yes, the j7 and j7+ have front-facing cameras with PrecisionVision Navigation that can detect and avoid obstacles like cables and pet waste.

faq.q: Will Error 26 damage my Roomba?
faq.a: No, the error is a protective stop. Repeatedly bumping into objects will not damage the robot.

title: Battery Not Connected
shortDescription: Roomba cannot detect the battery or the battery is not making proper contact.
fullDescription: Charging Error 1 indicates that the main board cannot detect the battery. This is usually caused by the battery being disconnected, having corroded contacts, or being at the end of its lifespan. The battery connector may also have come loose from vibration during use.

causes.cause: Battery connector loose or disconnected
Corroded battery contacts
Battery has reached end of life
Main board battery port damaged

symptoms: Charging Error 1 in the app or spoken by Roomba
Roomba will not charge on the Home Base
Battery indicator shows zero or unknown
Roomba turns off immediately after undocking

fixSteps.title: Reseat the battery
fixSteps.instruction: Flip Roomba over and remove the bottom plate screws. Carefully lift the plate and locate the battery. Disconnect and reconnect the battery connector firmly.

fixSteps.title: Clean battery contacts
fixSteps.instruction: Use isopropyl alcohol and a cotton swab to clean the battery contacts and the mating connector on the main board. Remove any corrosion or residue.

fixSteps.title: Test on the Home Base
fixSteps.instruction: Reassemble Roomba and place it on the Home Base. A solid green light on the Home Base indicates charging has begun. Wait 3 hours for a full charge.

fixSteps.title: Replace the battery
fixSteps.instruction: If the battery is old (3+ years) or swollen, replace it with a genuine iRobot battery matching your model.

whenToCallPro: If the battery connector port on the main board is physically damaged.
videoSearchQuery: Roomba charging error 1 battery fix replacement

faq.q: How long does a Roomba battery last?
faq.a: Typically 2-4 years depending on usage frequency. Battery capacity gradually decreases over time.

faq.q: Can I use third-party batteries?
faq.a: Third-party batteries are available at lower cost but may not have the same safety certifications. iRobot genuine batteries are recommended.

faq.q: Is a swollen battery dangerous?
faq.a: Yes. A swollen lithium battery is a safety hazard. Remove it carefully and dispose of it at a battery recycling facility. Do not puncture it.

title: Charging Timeout
shortDescription: Roomba's battery has not reached full charge within the expected time.
fullDescription: Charging Error 2 means the battery has been charging for longer than the maximum allowable time without reaching a full charge. This indicates the battery cannot hold a charge, the charger is not providing adequate current, or there is a connection problem.

causes.cause: Battery at end of life, cannot hold full charge
Dirty charging contacts on robot or base
Home Base power adapter not providing enough current
Ambient temperature too hot or too cold for charging

symptoms: Charging Error 2 in the app
Roomba sits on the base for hours but never shows full charge
Battery indicator never reaches 100 percent
Cleaning runs are much shorter than normal

fixSteps.title: Clean charging contacts
fixSteps.instruction: Wipe the two metal charging contacts on Roomba's underside and on the Home Base with a dry microfiber cloth. Ensure they are shiny and free of dust.

fixSteps.title: Check the power adapter
fixSteps.instruction: Ensure the Home Base power adapter is fully plugged into a working outlet. Try a different outlet. Check that the adapter cable is not damaged.

fixSteps.title: Move to a temperate area
fixSteps.instruction: Roomba batteries charge best between 40-95F (4-35C). Move the Home Base away from direct sunlight, heating vents, or cold areas.

fixSteps.title: Replace the battery
fixSteps.instruction: If Roomba is more than 2-3 years old and runtime has decreased significantly, the battery needs replacement.

whenToCallPro: If the charging circuit on the main board is faulty, preventing the battery from charging even with a new battery.
videoSearchQuery: Roomba charging error 2 battery timeout fix

faq.q: How long should Roomba take to fully charge?
faq.a: A full charge takes approximately 2-3 hours for lithium ion batteries and 3-4 hours for NiMH batteries.

faq.q: Can extreme temperatures affect charging?
faq.a: Yes. Lithium batteries charge very slowly or not at all below 32F (0C) and may overheat above 104F (40C).

faq.q: My Roomba only runs for 20 minutes, is the battery bad?
faq.a: If runtime has dropped significantly, the battery is likely degraded and needs replacement. Normal runtime is 60-120 minutes depending on the model.

title: Charging System Internal Error
shortDescription: An internal fault in the charging circuit has been detected.
fullDescription: Charging Error 3 indicates a hardware fault in Roomba's internal charging circuit. The charger IC or related components are not functioning correctly, preventing normal battery charging.

causes.cause: Temporary charging circuit glitch
Damaged charging port on Roomba
Faulty charging IC on the main board
Power surge damaged charging components

symptoms: Charging Error 3 in the app
Roomba does not charge on any base or charger
No indicator lights when placed on the base
Error appears immediately when docking

fixSteps.title: Reboot the robot
fixSteps.instruction: Remove Roomba from the base. Hold the CLEAN button for 10 seconds to reboot. Place it back on the base.

fixSteps.title: Try a different charging method
fixSteps.instruction: If you have the wall charger accessory, try charging directly with that instead of the Home Base to isolate the issue.

fixSteps.title: Inspect charging contacts
fixSteps.instruction: Check Roomba's underside charging contacts for any physical damage, burn marks, or deformation.

fixSteps.title: Contact iRobot support
fixSteps.instruction: Charging Error 3 is typically a hardware fault. Contact iRobot support for warranty service or repair options.

whenToCallPro: If a reboot does not resolve the error, professional repair is likely needed.
videoSearchQuery: Roomba charging error 3 internal fix

faq.q: Is Charging Error 3 fixable at home?
faq.a: Usually not. After a reboot, if the error persists, it indicates a hardware fault on the main board that requires professional repair.

faq.q: Can a power surge cause this error?
faq.a: Yes. Using a surge protector for the Home Base is recommended to protect the charging circuit.

faq.q: How much does it cost to fix Charging Error 3?
faq.a: If out of warranty, main board replacement typically costs between $100-250 including labor.

title: Charging Current Error
shortDescription: The charging system is receiving an abnormal current level.
fullDescription: Charging Error 5 indicates that the charging circuit is detecting an abnormal current flow during charging. The current may be too high (overcurrent) or too low, suggesting a problem with the battery, charging contacts, or power supply.

causes.cause: Corroded or damaged charging contacts
Failing battery with abnormal internal resistance
Home Base power adapter providing wrong voltage
Short circuit in battery or charging wiring

symptoms: Charging Error 5 in the app
Roomba charges intermittently
Charging contact area is warm to the touch
Burning smell from the charging area

fixSteps.title: Clean charging contacts thoroughly
fixSteps.instruction: Use isopropyl alcohol and a clean cloth to thoroughly clean both Roomba's and the Home Base's charging contacts. Remove all corrosion and debris.

fixSteps.title: Inspect for physical damage
fixSteps.instruction: Look carefully at the charging contacts for burns, melting, or deformation. If contacts are damaged, the affected component needs replacement.

fixSteps.title: Try a different Home Base
fixSteps.instruction: If available, try a different Home Base or the direct wall charger to isolate whether the issue is the base or the robot.

fixSteps.title: Replace the battery
fixSteps.instruction: An old battery with abnormal internal resistance can cause current irregularities. Install a new battery and test.

whenToCallPro: If you notice burning smells or visible damage to internal wiring, stop using the robot immediately and seek professional repair.
videoSearchQuery: Roomba charging error 5 current fix contacts

faq.q: Is a warm charging area normal?
faq.a: Slight warmth during charging is normal. However, if the contacts are hot to the touch or smell burnt, stop using the robot immediately.

faq.q: Can dirty contacts cause charging errors?
faq.a: Yes, dirty or corroded contacts create resistance, which causes abnormal current flow and triggers Charging Error 5.

faq.q: Should I be worried about a fire risk?
faq.a: If you smell burning or see melted plastic, stop using the product and contact iRobot support immediately. Keep it away from flammable materials.

title: Battery Overheated During Charging
shortDescription: The battery temperature has exceeded the safe charging threshold.
fullDescription: Charging Error 6 occurs when the battery thermistor reports that the battery temperature has exceeded the safe limit during charging. Charging is suspended to prevent battery damage or safety hazards.

causes.cause: Home Base or robot in direct sunlight or near heat source
Ambient room temperature too high
Battery degraded and generating excess heat during charge
Battery thermistor sensor faulty

symptoms: Charging Error 6 in the app
Roomba warm or hot to the touch
Charging stops after a short time
Error occurs during warm weather

fixSteps.title: Cool down the robot
fixSteps.instruction: Remove Roomba from the Home Base and place it in a cool, shaded area for 30-60 minutes to allow the battery to cool.

fixSteps.title: Move the Home Base
fixSteps.instruction: Relocate the Home Base away from direct sunlight, heating vents, and warm appliances. A temperature-controlled area between 50-80F (10-27C) is ideal.

fixSteps.title: Retry charging
fixSteps.instruction: After cooling, place Roomba back on the Home Base. Charging should resume normally.

fixSteps.title: Replace aging battery
fixSteps.instruction: Batteries that are old or have degraded cells generate more heat during charging. Replace the battery if the error recurs in cool conditions.

whenToCallPro: If the battery is visibly swollen or the error occurs even in cool conditions with a new battery.
videoSearchQuery: Roomba charging error 6 battery overheating fix

faq.q: Is it normal for Roomba to get warm during charging?
faq.a: Mild warmth is normal. However, if the robot is hot to the touch, the battery may be degraded.

faq.q: At what temperature does Charging Error 6 trigger?
faq.a: The exact threshold is not published, but generally above 113F (45C) battery temperature.

faq.q: Can I use Roomba immediately after it has been in the sun?
faq.a: Let it cool down in a shaded area for at least 30 minutes before running or charging.

title: Battery Not Warming During Charge
shortDescription: The battery temperature is not rising as expected during the charging process.
fullDescription: Charging Error 7 indicates that the battery thermistor is not detecting the expected temperature rise during charging. This may mean the battery is not accepting charge, the thermistor has failed, or there is a break in the charging circuit.

causes.cause: Battery cells are dead and cannot accept charge
Battery thermistor sensor disconnected
Charging circuit not delivering power to battery
Extremely cold ambient temperature

symptoms: Charging Error 7 in the app
Battery percentage never increases
Roomba feels cold after sitting on charger
Charging light pattern is abnormal

fixSteps.title: Warm up the environment
fixSteps.instruction: If the room is cold (below 50F/10C), move the Home Base and Roomba to a warmer room and try charging again.

fixSteps.title: Reboot and try again
fixSteps.instruction: Hold CLEAN for 10 seconds to reboot. Place back on the Home Base and monitor charging.

fixSteps.title: Replace the battery
fixSteps.instruction: A battery that cannot accept charge has reached end of life. Replace with a new battery.

fixSteps.title: Check battery thermistor
fixSteps.instruction: If a new battery still triggers this error, the thermistor wire on the battery connector may be damaged. Inspect the battery connector for all pins being intact.

whenToCallPro: If a new battery still triggers the error, the charging circuit on the main board needs inspection.
videoSearchQuery: Roomba charging error 7 battery not charging fix

faq.q: What does it mean if the battery feels cold on the charger?
faq.a: If the battery feels cold after an hour on the charger, it is likely not accepting any charge. The battery needs replacement.

faq.q: Can cold weather kill a Roomba battery?
faq.a: Storing a lithium battery in freezing conditions for extended periods can permanently damage the cells. Store Roomba in a heated area.

faq.q: How do I know if it is the battery or the charger?
faq.a: Try charging with a different base or wall charger. If the error persists with a different charger, the battery is the issue.

title: Vacuum Not Communicating With Base
shortDescription: Roomba cannot establish communication with the Home Base or Clean Base.
fullDescription: Charging Error 8 indicates a communication failure between Roomba and the Home Base or Clean Base. While power may be reaching the robot, the data communication handshake has failed, preventing proper charging management.

causes.cause: Dirty or corroded communication contacts
Home Base firmware outdated
Clean Base communication board fault
Roomba docking misalignment

symptoms: Charging Error 8 in the app
Clean Base does not empty the dustbin
Roomba sits on base but charging indicator is abnormal
Home Base light flashes error pattern

fixSteps.title: Clean all contacts
fixSteps.instruction: Thoroughly clean the charging contacts on both Roomba and the Home Base/Clean Base with isopropyl alcohol and a clean cloth.

fixSteps.title: Redock Roomba
fixSteps.instruction: Remove Roomba from the base, wait 10 seconds, and dock it again, ensuring it seats properly and the contacts align.

fixSteps.title: Power cycle the Home Base
fixSteps.instruction: Unplug the Home Base from the wall for 60 seconds. Plug it back in and wait for it to initialize before docking Roomba.

fixSteps.title: Update firmware
fixSteps.instruction: With Roomba docked, check for firmware updates in the iRobot Home app. Both the robot and Clean Base may need updates.

whenToCallPro: If the Clean Base communication board has failed, it will need professional repair or replacement.
videoSearchQuery: Roomba charging error 8 clean base communication fix

faq.q: Does this error only happen with the Clean Base?
faq.a: It can occur with both the standard Home Base and the Clean Base, but it is more common with the Clean Base due to its more complex communication protocol.

faq.q: Will Roomba still charge with this error?
faq.a: It may charge partially but the intelligent charging management and auto-empty features will not work correctly.

faq.q: How do I update the Clean Base firmware?
faq.a: Dock Roomba on the Clean Base, ensure both are connected to Wi-Fi, and check for updates in the iRobot Home app.

title: Drive Wheels Spinning Without Traction
shortDescription: The drive wheels are spinning but Roomba is not moving forward.
fullDescription: Error 4 indicates that Roomba's drive wheels are rotating but the robot is not making forward progress. This is typically caused by wheels spinning on a slippery surface or one wheel being lifted off the ground.

causes.cause: Roomba is on a very slippery surface
One wheel is lifted off the ground by an object underneath
Wheel treads are worn smooth
Front caster wheel jammed, preventing forward motion

symptoms: Roomba beeps four times
Error 4 in the app
Audible wheel spinning sound without movement
Roomba stuck in one spot on smooth floors

fixSteps.title: Move Roomba to a different surface
fixSteps.instruction: Pick up Roomba and place it on a surface with more traction, such as carpet or a textured floor.

fixSteps.title: Check for objects underneath
fixSteps.instruction: Turn Roomba over and check that nothing is stuck underneath that could lift a wheel off the ground.

fixSteps.title: Clean the front caster wheel
fixSteps.instruction: Pull the front caster wheel out of its housing. Remove hair and debris from the axle and inside the housing. Snap it back in.

fixSteps.title: Check drive wheel tread
fixSteps.instruction: Inspect the rubber tread on both drive wheels. If the tread is smooth or peeling, replace the wheel modules.

whenToCallPro: Rarely needed. This is almost always an environmental issue.
videoSearchQuery: Roomba Error 4 wheels spinning no traction fix

faq.q: Can Roomba work on polished hardwood floors?
faq.a: Yes, but very polished or waxed floors can reduce traction. Roomba generally handles most floor types well.

faq.q: How often should I clean the front caster wheel?
faq.a: Clean the front caster wheel every two weeks. It picks up hair and debris that can impede rotation.

faq.q: What if the rubber wheel tread is peeling?
faq.a: Replace the wheel module. Peeling tread significantly reduces traction on hard floors.

title: Cliff Sensors Need Cleaning
shortDescription: One or more cliff sensors are not functioning correctly after multiple attempts.
fullDescription: Error 12 is an escalated cliff sensor error. Unlike Error 1 or 6, it indicates persistent cliff sensor failures across multiple cleaning attempts. The sensors may need more thorough cleaning or could be failing.

causes.cause: Heavy dust or residue buildup on cliff sensors
One or more cliff sensors partially blocked by debris
Cliff sensor starting to fail with age
Cliff sensor board connector loose

symptoms: Error 12 in the app
Repeated cliff errors across multiple cleaning attempts
Roomba refuses to start from any surface
Intermittent stopping during cleaning

fixSteps.title: Deep clean cliff sensors
fixSteps.instruction: Use isopropyl alcohol on a cotton swab to thoroughly clean all four cliff sensor windows on the underside of Roomba. Let dry completely.

fixSteps.title: Use compressed air
fixSteps.instruction: Blow compressed air into the cliff sensor recesses to dislodge any deep-seated dust or debris.

fixSteps.title: Test each sensor
fixSteps.instruction: Place Roomba on a light floor and start a cleaning run. Slowly wave your hand under each cliff sensor. Roomba should respond by stopping or changing direction, confirming each sensor works.

fixSteps.title: Check sensor connections
fixSteps.instruction: Remove the bottom plate and check the cliff sensor ribbon cable connection to the main board. Reseat it firmly.

whenToCallPro: If sensors remain unresponsive after thorough cleaning and connection checks.
videoSearchQuery: Roomba Error 12 cliff sensor deep cleaning fix

faq.q: How is Error 12 different from Error 1?
faq.a: Error 1 is a single cliff detection event. Error 12 indicates persistent cliff sensor issues that have occurred across multiple cleaning attempts.

faq.q: Can I replace individual cliff sensors?
faq.a: On most models, cliff sensors are on a shared board. You typically replace the entire cliff sensor assembly.

faq.q: Will dirty cliff sensors damage my Roomba?
faq.a: No, but they will prevent cleaning. Roomba stops as a safety measure to avoid falling down stairs.

title: Front Caster Wheel Stuck
shortDescription: The front caster wheel is not spinning freely.
fullDescription: Error 13 indicates that the front caster wheel is jammed or not rotating. The front caster provides stability and smooth movement across surfaces. When it is stuck, Roomba cannot navigate properly.

causes.cause: Hair wrapped tightly around the caster axle
Debris lodged in the caster housing
Caster wheel cracked or deformed
Caster housing damaged

symptoms: Error 13 in the app
Roomba makes scraping sound while moving
Caster wheel does not spin freely by hand
Roomba pulls to one side during cleaning

fixSteps.title: Remove the caster wheel
fixSteps.instruction: Flip Roomba over. Pull the front caster wheel assembly straight out of its housing with a firm pull.

fixSteps.title: Clean the wheel and axle
fixSteps.instruction: Pull the wheel off the axle post. Remove all hair and debris from the axle, the wheel bore, and inside the housing cavity.

fixSteps.title: Test rotation
fixSteps.instruction: Reassemble the wheel on the axle and spin it. It should rotate freely in all directions.

fixSteps.title: Replace if damaged
fixSteps.instruction: If the wheel is cracked, flat-spotted, or the axle is bent, replace the entire front caster assembly.

whenToCallPro: Only if the caster housing in the body is cracked or damaged, requiring body panel repair.
videoSearchQuery: Roomba Error 13 front caster wheel stuck cleaning fix

faq.q: How often should the front caster be cleaned?
faq.a: Every 2 weeks or whenever you notice Roomba making unusual sounds while moving.

faq.q: Is the front caster different on different Roomba models?
faq.a: Most models use a similar design but sizes can vary. Check your model number when ordering replacements.

faq.q: Can I run Roomba without the front caster?
faq.a: No. The caster provides essential balance. Without it, Roomba will scrape the floor and cannot navigate.

title: Camera or Visual Navigation Failure
shortDescription: The visual navigation camera has encountered a hardware or software failure.
fullDescription: Error 20 indicates a failure in the vSLAM or visual navigation camera system. The camera cannot capture images for navigation, likely due to a hardware fault, severely dirty lens, or a cable disconnection.

causes.cause: Camera lens heavily obscured by dust or fingerprints
Camera ribbon cable loose or disconnected
Camera module hardware failure
Firmware corruption affecting camera driver

symptoms: Error 20 in the app
Roomba reverts to random navigation instead of systematic rows
Map cannot be generated or is blank
Camera area appears physically damaged

fixSteps.title: Clean the camera lens
fixSteps.instruction: Locate the navigation camera and wipe the lens with a dry microfiber cloth. Remove all dust, fingerprints, and residue.

fixSteps.title: Reboot the robot
fixSteps.instruction: Hold the CLEAN button for 10 seconds. Wait 30 seconds, then turn it back on.

fixSteps.title: Factory reset
fixSteps.instruction: Perform a factory reset to reload the camera firmware and drivers.

fixSteps.title: Check camera cable
fixSteps.instruction: Remove the top cover of Roomba and locate the camera ribbon cable. Ensure it is firmly connected to both the camera and the main board.

whenToCallPro: If the camera module itself has failed and needs hardware replacement.
videoSearchQuery: Roomba Error 20 camera navigation failure fix

faq.q: Can Roomba still clean without the camera?
faq.a: Roomba may revert to random bounce cleaning (like older models) but will lose smart mapping and room-specific features.

faq.q: Does the j7 have a different camera than the i7?
faq.a: Yes. The j7 has both a top-mounted navigation camera and a front-facing obstacle avoidance camera. Error 20 refers to the navigation camera.

faq.q: How much does a camera replacement cost?
faq.a: A replacement camera module typically costs $30-60 for the part. Professional installation adds $50-100.

title: Battery Authentication Error
shortDescription: Roomba cannot authenticate the installed battery.
fullDescription: Error 23 occurs when Roomba's main board cannot verify the battery's authentication chip. This can happen with non-genuine batteries that lack the authentication chip, or if the chip on a genuine battery is damaged.

causes.cause: Non-genuine battery installed without authentication chip
Battery authentication chip damaged
Battery connector not fully seated
Main board authentication circuit fault

symptoms: Error 23 in the app
Roomba refuses to charge or operate
Error appears immediately after battery replacement
Roomba worked with the original battery but not the replacement

fixSteps.title: Reseat the battery
fixSteps.instruction: Remove the bottom plate, disconnect the battery, wait 30 seconds, and reconnect it firmly.

fixSteps.title: Check battery compatibility
fixSteps.instruction: Verify you are using a battery compatible with your specific Roomba model. Some newer models require authenticated batteries.

fixSteps.title: Try an iRobot genuine battery
fixSteps.instruction: If using a third-party battery, try a genuine iRobot battery. Some models will only accept authenticated batteries.

fixSteps.title: Reboot the robot
fixSteps.instruction: With the battery connected, hold CLEAN for 10 seconds to force a reboot and re-attempt battery authentication.

whenToCallPro: If a genuine iRobot battery still triggers this error, the main board authentication circuit may be faulty.
videoSearchQuery: Roomba Error 23 battery authentication fix genuine battery

faq.q: Do all Roomba models require authenticated batteries?
faq.a: Newer i, j, and s series models have battery authentication. Older 600 and 800 series typically do not.

faq.q: Can I use a third-party battery?
faq.a: Some third-party batteries include compatible authentication chips. Check reviews and compatibility before purchasing.

faq.q: Is iRobot locking out third-party batteries?
faq.a: iRobot states that battery authentication is for safety verification. Some third-party manufacturers have adapted their batteries to include compatible chips.

title: Obstacle Avoidance Sensor Error (j7 Series)
shortDescription: The front-facing obstacle avoidance camera is not functioning properly.
fullDescription: Error 24 is specific to Roomba models with PrecisionVision Navigation (j7, j7+, j9). The front-facing camera used for obstacle detection and avoidance is not working. This does not affect basic navigation but removes the ability to avoid small obstacles.

causes.cause: Front camera lens dirty or smudged
Front camera LED illuminator failure
Camera ribbon cable disconnected
Front camera module failure

symptoms: Error 24 in the app
Roomba no longer avoids small obstacles like cables and shoes
Obstacle avoidance feature grayed out in app settings
Roomba bumps into objects it previously avoided

fixSteps.title: Clean the front camera
fixSteps.instruction: Wipe the front-facing camera lens and surrounding LEDs with a dry microfiber cloth. These are located on the front face of the bumper.

fixSteps.title: Reboot the robot
fixSteps.instruction: Hold CLEAN for 10 seconds to reboot. This resets the camera subsystem.

fixSteps.title: Update firmware
fixSteps.instruction: Check for firmware updates. Obstacle detection improvements are frequently included in updates.

fixSteps.title: Contact support
fixSteps.instruction: If the front camera LED does not illuminate during operation, the camera module may have failed and needs professional replacement.

whenToCallPro: If the camera module LED is not illuminating or the camera has physically failed.
videoSearchQuery: Roomba j7 Error 24 front camera obstacle avoidance fix

faq.q: Can I still use Roomba without obstacle avoidance?
faq.a: Yes, Roomba will still clean and navigate using its other sensors. However, it will bump into small objects instead of avoiding them.

faq.q: How does the front camera work in the dark?
faq.a: The j7 has built-in LEDs that illuminate the area in front of the camera, allowing obstacle detection even in dark rooms.

faq.q: Will Error 24 go away on its own?
faq.a: Sometimes, if it was caused by a temporary software glitch. A reboot usually resolves intermittent occurrences.

title: Mop Pad or Mopping System Error
shortDescription: The mopping pad or system is not detected or is malfunctioning (Combo models).
fullDescription: Error 25 applies to Roomba Combo models with mopping capability. The system cannot detect the mop pad, the pad lifting mechanism is jammed, or the water tank is not properly installed.

causes.cause: Mop pad not properly attached
Water tank not installed or empty
Mop lifting mechanism jammed
Mop pad detection sensor failure

symptoms: Error 25 in the app
Roomba Combo refuses to start mopping
Mop pad does not lift when transitioning to carpet
Water is not dispensed during mopping

fixSteps.title: Reattach the mop pad
fixSteps.instruction: Remove and reattach the mop pad, ensuring it snaps or adheres firmly to the mop plate.

fixSteps.title: Check the water tank
fixSteps.instruction: Remove the water tank, fill it with clean water, and reinsert it firmly until it clicks.

fixSteps.title: Clean the mop plate area
fixSteps.instruction: Wipe the mop plate and surrounding area to remove any debris that might prevent proper pad detection or attachment.

fixSteps.title: Test the lifting mechanism
fixSteps.instruction: Manually check if the mop pad lifts and lowers smoothly. If it feels stuck or grinds, debris may be in the mechanism.

whenToCallPro: If the mop lifting mechanism motor has failed.
videoSearchQuery: Roomba Combo j7 Error 25 mop pad fix

faq.q: How often should I replace the mop pad?
faq.a: Wash the reusable mop pad after each use. Replace it every 2-3 months or when it shows signs of wear.

faq.q: Can I use third-party mop pads?
faq.a: Some third-party pads are compatible, but they may not trigger the pad detection sensor correctly.

faq.q: Why does the mop pad not lift on my carpet?
faq.a: The mop pad auto-lift feature uses the map data to identify carpeted areas. Ensure your map is accurate and the carpet areas are properly labeled.

title: Battery Voltage Error
shortDescription: The battery voltage is outside the expected range during charging.
fullDescription: Charging Error 4 indicates that the battery voltage measured during charging is abnormally high or low. This can indicate a defective battery cell, a battery that has been deeply discharged, or a faulty voltage measurement circuit.

causes.cause: Battery deeply discharged from long period of non-use
One or more battery cells have failed
Battery has been stored in extreme temperatures
Voltage sensing circuit on main board faulty

symptoms: Charging Error 4 in the app
Battery was completely dead before placing on charger
Robot has not been used for months
Roomba powers off immediately when removed from base

fixSteps.title: Leave on charger for 72 hours
fixSteps.instruction: Place Roomba on the Home Base and leave it for up to 72 hours. Deeply discharged batteries sometimes need extended time to begin accepting charge.

fixSteps.title: Reseat the battery
fixSteps.instruction: Remove and reinstall the battery. Sometimes the connection needs to be refreshed for the voltage reading to normalize.

fixSteps.title: Replace the battery
fixSteps.instruction: If the battery has been in storage for over a year or is more than 3 years old, replace it. Lithium batteries lose capacity permanently when deeply discharged.

fixSteps.title: Check Home Base output
fixSteps.instruction: Using a multimeter, check the voltage at the Home Base contacts. It should read approximately 22V DC for lithium models.

whenToCallPro: If a new battery still shows this error, the main board voltage circuit may need repair.
videoSearchQuery: Roomba charging error 4 battery voltage fix deep discharge

faq.q: Can a deeply discharged battery be revived?
faq.a: Sometimes. Leaving it on the charger for 72 hours may revive it. However, capacity will likely be permanently reduced.

faq.q: How should I store Roomba long-term?
faq.a: Charge the battery to about 50 percent, then store Roomba in a cool, dry place. Charge it every 3-6 months to prevent deep discharge.

faq.q: What voltage should a healthy Roomba battery show?
faq.a: A fully charged lithium Roomba battery should read approximately 16-17V. Below 12V indicates deep discharge.
